ELPAC Level 2
Lexile BR–300L
DTR · TALK ABOUT A SCENE

Chasqui at Six O'Clock

STS-MS7-L2-BR300-MSSS7-BNB-LAT-01 • MS Social Studies Grade 7 • Meso-American and Andean Civilizations
Look at the picture. You will answer some questions about it. Press the play button to hear each question. Record your answer for each question. You can listen to your answer and record it again. Click SUBMIT RECORDINGS when you have answered all the questions.
JUST FOR FUN · OPTIONAL
Reporters race to explain big news in a few clear sentences, and the Inca had their own way of carrying news fast. Runners called chasquis sprinted in relays along 25,000 miles of stone road, passing knotted cords called quipus from hand to hand. Far to the north, Aztec farmers grew food on floating gardens built right on the water. Every empire needed a way to move news, and every one of them invented its own.
If you had one minute of air time, which story would you lead with: the roads, the runners, or the gardens? Why?
Optional. You do not have to record this one.
1
What is Ximena doing?
Look at the picture. Say: Ximena is ____ on the paper. (writing / sleeping)
2
What is Diego holding?
Look at the picture. Say: Diego is holding a ____. (stick with cords / ball)
3
What is the teacher doing?
Look at the picture. Say: The teacher is ____ the map. (measuring / washing)
4
What room is this? How do you know?
Look at the picture. Say: This is a ____ studio. (news / dance)
5
Tell me about the news screen.
Look at the picture. Say: The screen shows three ____. (photos / doors)
6
What is Valeria wearing? What is she doing?
Look at the picture. Say: Valeria is ____ the camera. (using / painting)
Record an answer for each question, then submit.
Content alignment: CA HSS 7.7 — Meso-American and Andean Civilizations. ELD (Speaking — official ELPAC “Talk About a Scene”): PI.A.1, PII.B.3, PII.B.4, PII.B.5.
